Title: Synthetic restricted metadata visibility
Purpose: Verify that restricted metadata visibility is reported as bounded permission evidence instead of an unhandled analyzer failure.

Arrange.Action: Create a synthetic database principal without a login and grant only the analyzer execution right.
Arrange.CompletionSignal: The restricted principal exists without server-state permissions.

Act.Action: Execute the framework capability analyzer under the restricted database-user context.
Act.CompletionSignal: The analyzer returns valid structured permission evidence.

Observe.Scope: Only the registered synthetic database and one lightweight analysis class.

AlternativeEvidence: A capability row with unavailable or unverified permission status is sufficient when the overall JSON contract remains valid.
ExpectedFindings.AssertionBoundary: The exact number and ordering of restricted capabilities are version- and feature-dependent.

AlternativeEvidence: The Hyper-V Linux lane remains NOT_EXECUTED until an isolated compatible guest is available.

Cleanup.Action: Remove the isolated run-owned container and its exact registered resources.
Cleanup.CompletionSignal: The restricted principal disappears with the marker-protected run data directory.

KnownLimitations: The scenario proves graceful permission handling, not every possible permission combination.
External native-Linux and Hyper-V-Linux runtime evidence is tracked separately from repository validation.
